communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records concerning Gwinnett County Public Schools’ handling of Lifetouch, Shutterfly, school photography services, picture day, student photographs, student-data privacy, vendor review, parent or community concerns, and claims or concerns involving Jeffrey Epstein, the “Epstein Files,” Apollo Global Management, or Leon Black.

Please search for records from January 1, 2026 through the date this request is fulfilled.

I request the following records:

- Meeting records for any Board of Education, committee, administrative, parent, town hall, staff, vendor, IT/privacy, legal, procurement, or other meeting where Lifetouch, Shutterfly, school photography, picture day, student photos, student data, parent concerns, vendor review, Epstein-related concerns, Apollo Global Management, or Leon Black was discussed. This includes agendas, minutes, board packets, exhibits, superintendent reports, presentations, recordings, transcripts, public-comment submissions, speaker cards, sign-in sheets, and notes.

- Records sufficient to show whether Gwinnett County Public Schools considered, approved, rejected, canceled, postponed, paused, modified, continued, reviewed, rescheduled, or replaced any Lifetouch/Shutterfly picture day, class picture, school portrait, yearbook photo, student ID photo, senior portrait, graduation photo, sports photo, or other student photography services.

- Records reflecting any response by Board of Education members, the superintendent, administrators, principals, communications staff, procurement staff, legal counsel, or other district employees to parent, staff, public, media, or board concerns about Lifetouch, Shutterfly, school photography, Epstein-related claims, Apollo Global Management, Leon Black, student photographs, student data, or vendor trustworthiness.

- All communications with Lifetouch, Shutterfly, Shutterfly Lifetouch, LLC, Apollo Global Management, photography vendors, alternative vendors, legal counsel, insurance representatives, IT personnel, privacy or cybersecurity consultants, Georgia education officials, other school districts, board members, or district administrators concerning this issue.

- All communications or complaints received from parents, guardians, students, staff, board members, residents, public commenters, media, public officials, or community members concerning Lifetouch, Shutterfly, school photos, student photographs, picture day, student data, student privacy, vendor review, Epstein-related claims, Apollo Global Management, or Leon Black. This includes emails, letters, phone logs, voicemail recordings or transcriptions, text messages, social media comments, social media direct messages, website form submissions, handwritten notes, complaint-tracking records, and memoranda of verbal correspondence.

- All notices, letters, robocall scripts, text-message alerts, website posts, social media posts, talking points, FAQs, press statements, staff guidance, board responses, superintendent responses, school-level responses, or other communications prepared for or sent to families, staff, students, media, or the public regarding this issue.

- Any formal response, letter, statement, assurance, presentation, memorandum, or other record from Lifetouch, Shutterfly, or Shutterfly Lifetouch, LLC concerning concerns raised by the district, board, parents, staff, public, or media, including any statement that there was or was not evidence of misconduct, compromise of student information, misuse of student images, unauthorized access, unauthorized disclosure, breach, sharing, sale, transfer, retention, deletion, or improper handling of student photographs or student data.

- All contracts, purchase orders, invoices, amendments, service agreements, memoranda of understanding, privacy agreements, data-processing agreements, cancellation provisions, cancellation notices, termination notices, vendor files, bid records, procurement records, and correspondence involving Lifetouch, Shutterfly, or Shutterfly Lifetouch, LLC for the 2025–26 and 2026–27 school years.

- All records concerning any review of Lifetouch/Shutterfly or other student photography vendors, including vendor-vetting materials, procurement records, RFPs, RFQs, bids, quotes, scoring sheets, evaluations, cost estimates, recommendations, alternative-vendor options, vendor comparisons, and plans for future school photography services.

- All records concerning student-data privacy, FERPA, Georgia student-record privacy requirements, cybersecurity, image storage, image retention, image deletion, opt-out procedures, consent, directory information, access controls, or any alleged risk involving student photographs handled by Lifetouch/Shutterfly.

- Records reflecting any determination, assurance, analysis, or statement that there was or was not any breach, misuse, unauthorized access, unauthorized disclosure, sharing, sale, transfer, retention, deletion, sale of student images, use for artificial intelligence or facial recognition, or improper handling of student photographs or student data by Lifetouch/Shutterfly.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Tracey
